metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Gaja Mitra Scheme to reduce human-elephant conflict?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Assam government launched the Gaja Mitra Scheme to reduce human-elephant conflict. The scheme is launched in 8 high-risk districts of Assam. It includes community-based rapid response teams with 8 local members in 80 conflict-prone villages. These teams will work during peak conflict months, especially paddy season, to guide elephant movement and protect crops.

questions.question: Which ministry has launched the ADEETIE scheme to boost industrial competitiveness, jobs, and climate action?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Ministry of Power launched the ADEETIE scheme to boost industrial competitiveness, jobs, and climate action. ADEETIE stands for Assistance in Deploying Energy Efficient Technologies in Industries & Establishments. It is a flagship scheme by the Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) under the Ministry of Power. The scheme targets Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) registered on the Udyam portal. It promotes adoption of energy-efficient technologies with at least 10% energy saving potential.

questions.question: The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) scheme was launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) has completed 10 years of implementation in 2025. It was launched in 2015 by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) to promote skill development in India. Currently in its fourth phase, Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) 4.0 is part of the Central Sector Scheme of the Skill India Programme.

questions.question: Which organization has launched the Prime Minister Professorship Scheme?

options.label: A
options.text: Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)

options.label: C
options.text: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

questions.explanation: The Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) launched the Prime Minister Professorship scheme to boost research in emerging state universities. It targets institutions with underdeveloped research ecosystems under ANRF’s PAIR (Partnerships for Advancing Interdisciplinary Research) program. The scheme enables eminent and superannuated scientists, faculty, and industry experts to mentor faculty and students, promote interdisciplinary research, and set up R&D labs. Each Professor receives ₹30 lakh/year fellowship, ₹24 lakh/year research grant, and ₹1 lakh/year overhead for the host university.

questions.question: The Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is an initiative of which ministry?
questions.explanation: Recently, Union Minister for Culture and Tourism Gajendra Singh Shekhawat shared updates on Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) in Rajya Sabha. Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. It aims to foster scientific temper and awareness among the public and students. National Council of Science Museums (NCSM) is the nodal agency for implementation. SPoCS supports Science Cities, Science Centres, Innovation Hubs, and Digital Planetariums. As of July 2025, 27 Science Centres have been set up across India. These centres offer hands-on exhibits, workshops, and outreach to promote science.

questions.question: What is the new name of the Employment Linked Incentive (ELI) Scheme?
questions.explanation: The Employment Linked Incentive (ELI) Scheme has been renamed as PM Viksit Bharat Rozgar Yojana (PM-VBRY) and will start from 1st August 2025. It aligns with the Viksit Bharat initiative to promote inclusive and sustainable employment. The scheme was approved by the Union Cabinet chaired by Prime Minister of India. PM-VBRY aims to create over 3.5 crore new jobs across India in two years. Out of these, 1.92 crore will be first-time workers entering the formal workforce. The scheme has a financial outlay of ₹99,446 crore. It will apply to jobs created between 1st August 2025 and 31st July 2027.

questions.question: Which institution has launched Veer Parivar Sahayata Yojana to strengthen legal aid for defence personnel and their families?
questions.explanation: The National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) recently launched the Veer Parivar Sahayata Yojana during the North Zone Regional Conference in Srinagar. The conference was themed “Reaffirming the Constitutional Vision of Justice for Defence Personnel and Tribals.” The scheme aims to strengthen legal aid services for defence personnel and their families. It will build a dedicated legal support network across Sainik Boards at Zilla, Rajya, and Kendra levels. These Sainik Boards will now also help families of soldiers with legal issues like land disputes, service entitlements, and civil matters.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the PM Ekta Mall initiative?

options.label: C
options.text: Promote and sell One District One Product and GI-tagged products

answer.label: C
answer.text: Promote and sell One District One Product and GI-tagged products

questions.explanation: Recently, 27 states—except West Bengal—received central approval to set up PM Ekta Malls under the One District, One Product (ODOP) initiative. PM Ekta Mall was announced in Union Budget 2023–24 to promote ODOP, Geographical Indication (GI) goods, and local handicrafts. The scheme is implemented under Part-VI of Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment (SASCI) by the Ministry of Finance. ₹4,796 crore worth of projects were approved in FY 2023–24 under this scheme. Uttar Pradesh received the highest ₹370.25 crore for malls in Agra, Lucknow, and Varanasi.

questions.question: Swasthya Sathi is a health insurance scheme launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: Recently, the West Bengal government included Bone Marrow Transplant (BMT) under its Swasthya Sathi universal health coverage scheme. Bone Marrow Transplant (BMT) is a procedure that replaces damaged or diseased bone marrow with healthy stem cells. Stem cells can come from the same person (autologous) or a donor (allogeneic). Swasthya Sathi is a health insurance scheme launched on 30 December 2016 by the West Bengal government. It offers cashless, paperless treatment coverage of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Annadata Sukhibhava scheme?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu launched the Annadata Sukhibhava-PM Kisan scheme in East Veerayapalem, Prakasam district, Andhra Pradesh. A total of ₹3,175 crore was disbursed to 46.85 lakh farmers, with ₹7,000 per farmer in the first phase. The ₹7,000 included ₹5,000 from the State (₹2,343 crore) and ₹2,000 from the Centre (₹832 crore), part of a ₹20,000 annual aid. The scheme is a historic step to empower farmers. Farmers were advised to use the Sukhibhava portal or helpline (155251) for any payment issues.
